homepage.heroSubline: Keeping Sacramento landscapes thriving through every season. We design irrigation systems that work with our local climate, not against it.
aboutText: Founded in 2012, Apex Irrigation began as a two-person operation servicing Midtown bungalows. Today, our team of 20+ specialists maintains over 800 systems across Sacramento County.
We approach irrigation as an environmental partnership—conserving water while accounting for clay soil shifts, extreme heat, and Sacramento's unique microclimates.
From sponsoring Water Wise workshops to volunteering with local urban greening projects, we're invested in Sacramento's sustainable future.

homepage.ctaSubline: Let's assess your current setup and discuss solutions tailored to your property.

services.name: Drip Irrigation Installation
services.shortDescription: Precision watering solutions for Sacramento's drought-prone landscapes.
services.description:
In Sacramento's climate, traditional sprinklers waste water through evaporation and runoff. Our drip systems deliver water directly to plant roots with 90% efficiency—critical during summer water restrictions.

We design zone-specific layouts for mixed landscaping, using pressure-compensating emitters that handle our variable water pressure. For established East Sacramento gardens, we retrofit existing systems with minimal disruption. In new Natomas developments, we install during construction for seamless integration.

Every installation includes a smart controller programmed with Sacramento's watering schedules, plus leak detection to prevent costly clay soil erosion.
process: Step 1: Site assessment of sun exposure, soil type, and plant water needs
Step 2: Custom design with water-efficient emitter placement
Step 3: Installation with freeze-proof components for mild winters
Step 4: Smart controller programming with seasonal adjustments
benefits: Reduces water use by 30-50% compared to sprinklers
Prevents overspray on Sacramento's narrow lots
Eliminates runoff on compacted clay soils
Qualifies for Sacramento County rebates

faq.question: How does drip irrigation handle Sacramento's hard water?
faq.answer: We install self-flushing emitters and recommend annual descaling to prevent mineral buildup from our groundwater supply.

faq.question: Can drip systems water lawns in Land Park homes?
faq.answer: For turf areas, we use subsurface drip lines or recommend converting to drought-tolerant landscaping better suited to our climate.

services.name: Sprinkler System Repair
services.shortDescription: Fast fixes for Sacramento's most common irrigation failures.
services.description:
Sacramento's temperature swings and soil movement wreak havoc on irrigation systems. We specialize in diagnosing and repairing the issues unique to our area—from cracked PVC in expanding clay to sun-damaged valves in 110°F heat.

Our mobile crews carry parts for quick fixes to minimize water waste, including Sacramento-specific solutions like reinforced swing joints for unstable soil. For older Midtown systems, we identify outdated spray heads that violate current water regulations.

Repairs include a full system evaluation with digital pressure testing and a detailed report showing potential future failure points—particularly important for historic homes with aging infrastructure.
process: Step 1: Digital pressure testing at all zones
Step 2: Inspection of heads, valves, and pipes for heat/soil damage
Step 3: Repair with UV-resistant and soil-movement tolerant components
Step 4: Adjustment for current Sacramento watering restrictions
benefits: Same-day service for critical leaks
Extended warranty on all repair parts
Compliance documentation for water district audits
Prevents costly water bills from undetected leaks

faq.question: Why does my system lose pressure every summer?
faq.answer: Sacramento's peak water demand often lowers municipal pressure. We install booster pumps or redesign zones to compensate.

faq.question: How often should older systems be inspected?
faq.answer: For pre-1990s systems common in East Sac, we recommend biannual checks—before summer and after winter rains.

services.name: Smart Controller Upgrades
services.description:
Outdated timers waste thousands of gallons watering during Sacramento's summer heatwaves. Our smart controllers sync with local weather stations and soil moisture sensors, automatically adjusting for actual conditions.

We install Wi-Fi enabled models that integrate with Sacramento Regional Water Authority's rebate programs, including models that receive direct ET (evapotranspiration) data from UC Davis. For larger properties in Folsom's hillside communities, we configure multi-zone controllers that account for varying sun exposure.

Every upgrade includes personalized training—we know Sacramento homeowners range from tech-savvy young professionals to retirees preferring simple interfaces—plus remote monitoring for the first 90 days.
process: Step 1: Evaluation of existing wiring and compatibility
Step 2: Selection of appropriate model for property size and user preference
Step 3: Professional installation with surge protection
Step 4: Hands-on training and smartphone app setup
benefits: Automatically complies with Sacramento watering restrictions
Reduces water use by 20-40%
Qualifies for up to $150 in local rebates
Remote control via smartphone when traveling

faq.question: Will this work during Sacramento's power fluctuations?
faq.answer: All controllers include battery backup and surge protection for our area's common brownouts.

faq.question: How does it handle sudden rainstorms?
faq.answer: Models with wireless rain sensors override schedules instantly—critical for our unpredictable winter weather.

services.name: Backflow Prevention Services
services.shortDescription: Mandatory protection for Sacramento's drinking water.
services.description:
California law requires annual backflow testing to prevent irrigation contaminants from entering Sacramento's water supply. Our certified testers handle everything from 1920s bungalow systems to large commercial installations.

We specialize in the complex valve assemblies common in Sacramento's older neighborhoods, where aging pipes increase contamination risks. Testing includes full documentation for submission to SMUD and the City of Sacramento—we know exactly which forms each jurisdiction requires.

For failed tests, we carry all common repair parts on our trucks to minimize downtime. We also install new assemblies to current code standards, particularly important for homes undergoing renovation in Midtown's historic districts.
process: Step 1: Visual inspection of assembly for code compliance
Step 2: Pressure testing with calibrated gauges
Step 3: Complete documentation for water provider submission
Step 4: Immediate repairs if test fails
benefits: Keeps your system legally operational
Prevents $500+ fines for non-compliance
Same-day documentation submission
Certified testing accepted by all local agencies

faq.question: Why does Sacramento require annual testing?
faq.answer: Our clay soil's shifting can misalign valves over time, while older pipes have higher contamination risks.

faq.question: My test failed—what's the next step?
faq.answer: We typically repair or replace assemblies same-day using parts that meet Sacramento's strict lead-free requirements.

services.name: Irrigation Winterization
services.shortDescription: Protecting Sacramento systems from rare but damaging freezes.
services.description:
While Sacramento winters are mild, overnight frosts can crack pipes and valves—especially problematic in older systems with brittle components. Our winterization service blows out all water using industrial air compressors, reaching deep into Sacramento's common multi-zone systems.

We pay special attention to vulnerable areas like backflow preventers in unheated garages and exposed pipes in Natomas' newer developments. Service includes a full inspection to identify components that may need replacement before next season.

For clients with delicate landscaping, we install manual drain valves that allow partial system use during warm spells—a popular option for Land Park's historic rose gardens.
process: Step 1: Complete system shutdown and manual drainage
Step 2: Compressed air blowout of all lines and emitters
Step 3: Insulation of exposed backflow preventers
Step 4: Report noting components needing spring replacement
benefits: Prevents costly spring repairs from freeze damage
Identifies worn parts before they fail
Flexible scheduling throughout November
Priority status for spring startup

faq.question: When is the best time to winterize in Sacramento?
faq.answer: We recommend late October through November, before the first frost but after summer watering needs end.

faq.question: Do I need this if I have an automatic drain system?
faq.answer: Yes—auto drains often leave water in low spots where Sacramento's clay soil shifts have created new grades.

reviews.name: Marta K.
reviews.text: The team converted our entire Land Park yard to drip irrigation before the summer water restrictions hit. They perfectly balanced our heritage roses with new drought-tolerant plants, and our water bill dropped 40% while everything stays lush.

reviews.name: James R.
reviews.text: Apex fixed three broken lines in our East Sac home's ancient system—turns out the clay soil had shifted them to the breaking point. They reinforced everything with flexible connectors and even adjusted the spray pattern to stop watering our driveway.

reviews.name: Lien T.
reviews.text: Our new controller automatically skips watering when it's windy (which is always in Natomas). The app makes it easy to adjust when we're away, and the rebate processing was seamless.

reviews.name: Carlos M.
reviews.text: Fast and professional backflow testing for our four-plex in Midtown. They handled all the city paperwork and even spotted a valve issue we didn't know about. Done in under an hour.

reviews.name: Susan P.
reviews.text: After last year's freeze damaged our pipes, we won't skip winterization again. Apex's team was thorough explaining the process and left clear notes for spring startup. Worth every penny.

reviews.name: Damien L.
reviews.text: They designed an ingenious drip system for our sloped yard in Folsom that prevents runoff. The smart zoning keeps our oak trees happy while conserving water—exactly what we needed for this climate.

service-areas.title: Sacramento Irrigation Service Areas | Apex
service-areas.description: Professional irrigation services available throughout Sacramento County including Elk Grove, Roseville, and Folsom.

serviceAreas.content: Apex Irrigation provides expert irrigation solutions for Elk Grove's thriving master-planned communities. With summers often exceeding 100°F, efficient water management is critical for maintaining lush landscapes. Our tailored systems ensure your lawn and garden thrive while conserving water. From smart controllers to drought-resistant designs, we help Elk Grove homeowners optimize irrigation for the hot, dry climate. We specialize in modern irrigation installations and upgrades for homes built from the 1990s to today.
serviceAreas.localContext: Elk Grove is a fast-growing city with master-planned communities developed primarily from the 1990s onward.

serviceAreas.content: Roseville's beautiful planned communities deserve irrigation systems that match their quality. Apex Irrigation understands the unique needs of Roseville's landscapes in the intense summer heat. We design systems that account for water restrictions while keeping properties green and vibrant. Our team is experienced with the specific soil types and zoning regulations in Roseville's newer developments. Whether it's repairing an existing system or installing a new water-efficient design, we help Roseville residents maintain curb appeal year-round.
serviceAreas.localContext: Roseville features numerous planned communities developed primarily between the 1990s and 2000s.

We're familiar with all the latest water-saving technologies ideal for Folsom's modern developments.
